Scenario: Broad cosmic source + linear propagation effects
Horn result: Steady signal repeats unless propagation changes it
Interpretation: The time trace is easy to mimic, but adjacent channels remain almost equally bright.

Scenario: Intrinsically narrow cosmic transient + propagation effects
Horn result: Passes if it fades by >14.8 dB
Interpretation: Exponential fading needs tau < 50.4 s, or switch-off during the ~105 s window.

Scenario: Steady narrow celestial source with strong scintillation
Horn result: Can miss the second horn, but repeated independent follow-ups dominate
Verdict: Nearly rejected as a persistent source
Interpretation: Toy all-miss probability is 3.199e-05 after 3 independent epochs and 3.224e-08 after 5; scintillation only helps compact sources.

Scenario: 3-30 MHz OTH signals through linear or low-order nonlinear path
Interpretation: Second- and third-order positive products top out at 90 MHz.

Scenario: Very-high-order harmonic from a single OTH/HF carrier
Interpretation: At least harmonic order 48 is required from 30 MHz.

Scenario: Two strong UHF tones create IM2/IM3 in nonlinear electronics
Horn result: Conditional on nonlinear stage and sidelobe coupling
Interpretation: Radiometer-corrected target is -148.8 dBm; IIP3=-10 dBm needs ~-56.3 dBm per tone.

Scenario: Symmetric terrestrial RFI in both horn paths
Interpretation: Near-equal horn coupling requires an enormous common signal to leave 30.5 sigma residual.

Horn result: Can favor one feed by many dB
Verdict: Only weakly constrained by horn differencing
Interpretation: With 10-20 dB power-coupling asymmetry, the stronger path need only be about 31-34 sigma.

Scenario: Continuous narrow celestial source without propagation modulation
Interpretation: Without intrinsic intermittency or propagation modulation, it should have appeared in the second horn and in later observations.

Horn result: Can evade both horn and later observations
Verdict: Conditional; duty cycle depends on coverage
Interpretation: Allowed duty cycle depends strongly on independent epochs, coverage, sensitivity and drift-frequency coverage.
The 192-hour benchmark strongly disfavors frequent repeaters but still permits sufficiently rare events.

Scenario: Extended or broadband source invoking deep scintillation
Horn result: Deep scintillation is quenched by source extent
Interpretation: The scintillation escape hatch applies only to very compact sources and does not reopen the broadband/extended class.
